title: cercocarpus betuloides subsp. macrourus
intro: Cercocarpus betuloides subsp. macrourus, also known as the mountain mahogany, is a shrub or small tree species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cercocarpus betuloides subsp. macrourus include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
